risk_title: Security situation delays implementation in Nabatieh and Tyre service zones
risk_summary: Unstable security conditions in Nabatieh and Tyre are impacting data collection for Local Solid Waste Master Plans, requiring shift to virtual meetings and remote tools to mitigate risks.
evidence_quote: Given the unstable security situation in Nabatieh and Tyre, the mission recommended that data collection efforts are based on virtual meetings and remote tools where possible to mitigate security risks and ensure continuity of the work.

risk_title: Component 2 implementation jeopardized by security escalation
risk_summary: Security situation in Lebanon has caused delays in Component 2 activities, with potential to jeopardize project development objectives. Activities were previously put on hold due to escalation in security situation.
evidence_quote: Component 2 observed a slippage in implementation due to the security situation, its implementation may be jeopardizing the project development objectives.

risk_title: Stakeholder opposition and threats to Srar Sanitary Landfill project
risk_summary: A grievance was received from a landowner's brother operating an adjacent open dump who opposes the SLF and threatens to damage it if not included in the project. This competing demand must be resolved before civil works commence.
evidence_quote: The complainant opposes the SLF because is operates an open dump landfill on the adjacent land plot. He is insisting to be included in the project as well, otherwise he is threatening he will damage the SLF supported by the project. The mission communicated that these competing demands must be resolved by the MoE, Akkar Governor and the Union of Municipalities before any civil work start for the Srar SLF.

risk_title: ESIA for Nabatieh SLF delayed due to security issues
risk_summary: The Environmental and Social Impact Assessment for the Kfartebnit Sanitary Landfill in Nabatieh was delayed due to security issues, now under development since February 2025 with expected completion by August 2025.
evidence_quote: The ESIA for the Nabatieh SLF was delayed due to security issues, however it is currently under development since February 2025 and is expected to be finalized by August 2025.

risk_title: Conflict-related rubble management activity cannot be completed within project timeline
risk_summary: Management of asbestos-contaminated rubble at the Bakalian site from the 2023-2024 conflict cannot be completed within the project's timeline, requiring budget cancellation and deferral to future operations.
evidence_quote: The management of asbestos-contaminated rubble at the Bakalian site including its safe disposal cannot be completed within the project's timeline and therefore the budget allocated to this activity will be cancelled.

risk_title: Occupational health and safety violations at Karantina facility amid conflict recovery
risk_summary: Multiple serious OHS violations observed at Karantina Sorting Facility including inadequate PPE, unsecured work areas, and unreported incidents requiring hospitalization. Workers' safety at risk during critical post-conflict reconstruction work.
evidence_quote: Workers were not equipped with appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), and none were wearing safety shoes... OHS incidents resulting in emergency hospital visits were recorded in the incident log however they were not reported to the World Bank.

risk_title: Grievance mechanism gaps for workers at Karantina facility
risk_summary: Workers report grievances verbally on site and these are included in contractor reports, but UNDP does not record them in the grievance log or reports to the Bank, creating accountability gaps.
evidence_quote: During the site visit, it was noted that workers report grievance, feedback or complaints on site, often verbally and these are included in the Contractor's monthly reports, however UNDP does not record these in the grievance log and in the reports to the Bank.

risk_title: Land expropriation compensation delays affecting project beneficiaries
risk_summary: 52% of expropriated landowners (27 out of 50) have not collected compensation for land taken for Zahle sewer network, primarily due to absentee owners who left Lebanon and currency depreciation making compensation negligible.
evidence_quote: Remaining 13 plots, involving 27 landowners (52 %) still did not collect the compensation, mainly because the owners live abroad, the devaluation of the LBP decreased substantially the compensation amount in real terms, which combined with the complexity of the procedures to claim the compensation under the Lebanese Law left no incentive to collect the compensation.

risk_title: Conflict-driven economic crisis reducing landowner incentive to claim compensation
risk_summary: The 2019 economic crisis and Lebanese lira depreciation have reduced compensation amounts to negligible levels (USD 27-728 per plot), eliminating incentive for landowners to claim expropriation compensation.
evidence_quote: Of these, 10 plots were compensated before the onset of the 2019 economic crisis, while the owners of 2 plots collected compensation afterward... the devaluation of the LBP decreased substantially the compensation amount in real terms, which combined with the complexity of the procedures to claim the compensation under the Lebanese Law left no incentive to collect the compensation.
